pkg-004.title: Lynx BYOVD Kernel Exploit
pkg-004.subtitle: Vulnerable driver load → kernel exploit → EDR blind → privilege escalation

terminal_steps.text: ║   SCENARIO: LYNX BYOVD KERNEL EXPLOIT — pkg-004            ║
║   Vulnerable Driver → Kernel Exploit → EDR Bypass           ║
[SYSTEM] Loading scenario: Lynx BYOVD Kernel Exploit
[SYSTEM] Threat actor: Lynx (Ransomware group using BYOVD)
[SYSTEM] Signals: CrowdStrike Falcon, Windows Event Logs, Sysmon
━━━ PHASE 1: THE BODY — Driver Load Monitoring ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
[BODY] Signer: Micro-Star International (MSI) — VALID but KNOWN VULNERABLE
[BODY]   ├─ Used by: Lynx, BlackByte, AvosLocker, Lazarus
[BODY] Parent process: svchost.exe → sc.exe create RTCore64 binPath=...
[BODY] Post-load: IOCTL calls to patch kernel callback table (T1014)
[BRAIN]   ├─ Driver loaded from Temp directory: staging technique
[BRAIN]   ├─ IOCTL pattern: kernel callback patching → EDR blinding
[BRAIN]   └─ Threat actor fingerprint: 94% match → Lynx group
[BRAIN] Urgency: IMMEDIATE — EDR is being actively disabled
━━━ PHASE 3: THE PURPOSE — Driver Removal & Lockdown ━━━━━━━━
[CLAW]   target: crowdstrike_rtr | cmd: sc stop RTCore64 && sc delete RTCore64
[CLAW]   target: crowdstrike_api | action: force sensor reconnect
[CLAW]   └─ ✓ CrowdStrike kernel callbacks restored | Sensor: ACTIVE
[CLAW]   target: gpo_api | action: add RTCore64.sys hash to WDAC policy
[CLAW]   └─ ✓ Driver hash added to org-wide blocklist
━━━ PHASE 4: THE NERVOUS SYSTEM — Org-Wide Driver Defense ━━━
║   ✓ SCENARIO COMPLETE: Lynx BYOVD Kernel Exploit            ║
║   EDR Blind Window: 0.9s | Driver: Unloaded + Blocked       ║
║   Org-wide WDAC: Updated | Kernel Hooks: Restored           ║
[SYSTEM] Lab safety policy enforced. No production systems affected.
